title: 10 best herbal creams for colds

remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Purple coneflower's antiviral compounds, such as echinacoside and cinnamic acid, have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses like the common cold-causing rhinovirus, helping to reduce symptoms and duration of illness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The creams' anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages and throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving discomfort.

Immune system support: Purple coneflower's immunomodulatory effects, which involve enhancing the body's natural defense mechanisms, can help boost the immune system's response to infection, reducing the severity and duration of cold symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with colds because it contains unique compounds that stimulate the immune system. These active ingredients, including alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, boost the body's natural defenses, making it more resilient to viral infections. By reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being, Echinacea purpurea creams can alleviate cold symptoms, such as congestion, sore throats, and fatigue. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support immune function and relieve cold-related discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help alleviate cold symptoms by acting as anti-inflammatory agents, reducing the severity of respiratory infections and promoting overall well-being.

Flavonoids (including Kaempferol and Quercetin): These polyphenolic compounds exhibit strong antioxidant properties, which help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by viral infections, thus reducing the risk and severity of colds.
Alkylamides: These compounds have immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's natural immune response to fight off invading pathogens and reduce the duration and severity of cold symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used in creams for colds because they contain high concentrations of active compounds, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Flowers: They are used in creams for colds because they are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate cold symptoms.

Leaves: They are used in creams for colds because they contain compounds like caffeic acid and chicoric acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce cold symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested echinacea flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea flowers and leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of the echinacea infusion.
Stir the mixture constantly as it cools and thickens over a 30-minute period.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's primary benefit is its ability to thin mucus, making it easier to expel from the body, relieving congestion and coughing associated with colds.
Gaultheria procumbens (Wintergreen): Wintergreen contains methyl salicylate, a natural analgesic that provides pain relief for sore throats, headaches, and fever associated with colds.
Eucalyptus: Eucalyptus oil's decongestant properties help to ease bronchial congestion, making it easier to breathe when suffering from a cold or flu.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens, also known as wintergreen, creams helps with colds because it contains methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin. This compound helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion, making it easier to breathe and relax. Additionally, Gaultheria procumbens has antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat the underlying infection that causes colds. By soothing and calming the body, these creams can provide relief from cold symptoms, allowing for a more comfortable recovery.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, particularly peduncularidoid, found in Gaultheria procumbens, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help alleviate cold symptoms such as congestion and fever.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including gallic acid and ellagic acid, present in the herb, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help combat oxidative stress and reduce the severity of cold symptoms like cough and sore throat.

Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid, a triterpenoid saponin found in Gaultheria procumbens, has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate cold symptoms such as fever, cough, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce fever and relieve pain associated with colds.
Roots: They serve as the primary source of wintergreen oil, which is used in creams to provide a cooling sensation and relieve congestion.
Buds: They contain a high concentration of wintergreen oil, which is extracted and used in creams to help soothe sore throats and coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and flowers on a sunny morning when dew is still present.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the fresh plant material in 4 ounces of carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the oil mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture with an electric mixer until it becomes light and fluffy then transfer to a clean container.

remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Lemon balm cream's antiviral property helps to combat the viral infection responsible for the common cold, reducing its severity and duration.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce swelling and pain associated with a cold, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
Calming and soothing properties: Lemon balm's calming and soothing properties help to ease symptoms like coughing, sneezing, and runny nose, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with colds because it contains rosmarinic acid, a natural compound with potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, these properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the severity of cold symptoms such as sore throats and fever. Additionally, the calming effects of lemon balm can help to reduce stress and promote restful sleep, which is essential for the body to recover from illness. This natural remedy can provide relief and support during the cold and flu season.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate cold symptoms by reducing viral replication and inflammation in the body.

Luteolin: As a flavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, luteolin can help combat colds by reducing oxidative stress, modulating the immune response, and inhibiting the replication of viral pathogens.

Volatile oils (especially linalool and linalyl acetate): These terpenes possess antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the severity and duration of colds by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and alleviating associated inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are the most commonly used part in creams for colds, as they contain high concentrations of essential oils with decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Melissa officinalis flowers are also used due to their high content of melissic acid, which helps to soothe and calm respiratory issues associated with colds.

Leaves (again): The leaves of Melissa officinalis are often used in combination with other ingredients to create creams that not only relieve cold symptoms but also offer a calming effect to the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa leaves in late morning when the dew is evaporated but the heat has not become too strong.
Steam distill the melissa leaves to extract their essential oil and prepare the herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with a base ingredient such as beeswax and coconut oil to create a cream base.
Heat the cream base in a double boiler or a heat-resistant bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Cool the cream base and whip it to create a smooth and consistent texture before filling the containers.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to combat the underlying cause of colds.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses associated with colds.
Expectorant properties: The volatile oils in thyme help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm and reduce congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with colds because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Thyme contains compounds like carvacrol and thymol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause respiratory infections. When applied topically as a cream, thyme can help reduce congestion, soothe a sore throat, and alleviate coughs, making it a natural and effective remedy for cold and flu symptoms. Its invigorating scent also promotes relaxation and eases sinus pressure.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial properties that help combat the underlying bacterial infections that often accompany colds, reducing symptoms and the duration of illness.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol's decongestant and expectorant properties help relieve congestion and coughs associated with colds, making it easier to breathe and recover.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate cold symptoms by reducing inflammation and protecting the body from oxidative stress caused by the infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are used to make creams for colds because they are rich in thymol, a natural antiseptic and decongestant.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used to make creams for colds because they contain thymol and other volatile oils that have antimicrobial properties.
Buds: Thymus vulgaris buds are used to make creams for colds because they contain high amounts of thymol and other essential oils that help to relieve congestion and coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves in late summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Combine the dried herb with 100ml of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced to 50ml.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 20 grams of beeswax.
Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax has melted and then pour into a glass jar to cool.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Joe Pye Weed creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicin, which helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with colds, such as sinus pressure and congestion.

Antiviral properties: The plant contains antiviral compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which can help to combat the viral infection that causes colds, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Expectorant properties: Joe Pye Weed has a natural expectorant action, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieving coughing fits associated with colds.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eupatorium perfoliatum, also known as joe pye weed, creams helps with colds because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The herb contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been traditionally used to alleviate cold and flu symptoms, such as congestion and coughs. The creams may help to reduce swelling and ease the discomfort associated with a cold, allowing the body to recover more efficiently. Its soothing and calming effects also help to promote a restful night's sleep, which is essential for the body's immune system to fight off the infection.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Eupatorium perfoliatum creams may help with colds by exhibiting antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing the severity of symptoms.

Triterpenoids (specifically, Ursolic acid): Triterpenoids, particularly ursolic acid, found in Eupatorium perfoliatum may help alleviate cold symptoms by possessing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Saponins: Saponins present in Eupatorium perfoliatum creams may help with colds by exhibiting expectorant properties, making it easier to cough up mucus and reduce congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, contributes to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Eupatorium perfoliatum contain sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds that help in relieving congestion and reduce the severity of cold symptoms.
Stems: The stems of the plant are rich in iridoid glycosides, which possess anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, making them useful in cold remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated to prevent contamination.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks to remove moisture.
Combine 2 ounces of dried eupatorium perfoliatum with 4 ounces of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2-3 hours at 100-150 degrees fahrenheit to release the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Goldenrod contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses.

Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial properties, including triterpenoid saponins and caffeic acid esters, help to combat infections caused by cold viruses, reducing the severity of symptoms like coughs and sore throats.

Expectorant effects: Goldenrod's expectorant properties, primarily due to the presence of mucilage and other glycosides, help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviate congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Solidago virgaurea, also known as goldenrod, creams helps with colds because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids work together to reduce congestion, alleviate sore throats, and ease sinus pressure. Goldenrod's antimicrobial properties also help combat viral infections, while its expectorant properties promote the removal of mucus, allowing for easier breathing and a faster recovery from cold symptoms. This natural remedy provides relief and promotes overall well-being during cold and flu season.
remedy_constituents: Asteraceae saponins: These saponins have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate cold symptoms such as congestion and sore throats.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which can help combat the underlying infection that causes colds and reduce the severity of symptoms.
Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin): Quercetin has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity of cold symptoms such as congestion, coughing, and sore throats.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties to reduce swelling and ease congestion.
Stems: Utilized for their antimicrobial properties to combat infections and soothe sore throats.
Flowers: Employed for their expectorant properties to help clear mucus and ease coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried solidago virgaurea flowers and leaves on a sunny day for medicinal use.
Steep the herb in 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to separate the solids.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter with 100ml of the infused oil.
Stir the mixture over low heat until the beeswax melts and the mixture thickens into a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lavender oil's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause cold symptoms, reducing the severity of congestion and coughing.
Inflammation reduction: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender oil, such as linalool, alleviate sore throats and sinus pressure by reducing swelling and pain caused by infection.

Anxiolytic properties: Lavender's calming effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety associated with colds, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality to aid in the body's natural recovery process.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with colds because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to soothe sore throats and calm coughs. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to relieve congestion and promote a restful night's sleep, allowing the body to recover from the underlying infection. This natural remedy can provide relief and comfort during the cold and flu season.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the severity of cold symptoms, such as congestion and coughing.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has antiviral properties, which can help combat viral infections that cause colds, such as rhinovirus and influenza.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms of colds, such as sore throats and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high content of essential oils with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Used due to their ability to help soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes.
Seeds: Used due to their ability to provide a rich source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak potency and dryness for later use.
Infuse 250 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 1 liter of cold-pressed carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax and 200 grams of shea butter in a double boiler and melt at 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Gradually add the infused oil to the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture and whip until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Marigold's flavonoids, lutein, and zeaxanthin help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of colds such as congestion, sore throats, and sinus pressure.

Antiviral activity: Marigold contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses that cause the common cold, providing natural relief from viral infections.

Soften and protect mucus membranes: The mucilages present in marigold cream help to soften and protect mucus membranes in the nose, throat, and lungs, making it easier to expel excess mucus and reducing congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with colds because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, work together to soothe irritated skin, reduce swelling, and combat bacterial and viral infections that often accompany colds. Additionally, Calendula's ability to promote tissue repair and accelerate wound healing helps to alleviate congestion and ease respiratory symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with colds, such as sore throats and congestion.
Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, quercetin helps combat the oxidative stress and inflammation caused by viral infections, such as the common cold.

Alkaloids (specifically, calduloside): These compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties that help combat the underlying bacterial or viral infections causing cold symptoms, such as sinusitis or bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis are primarily used in creams for colds due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids that provide antiseptic and soothing properties, making them suitable for cold-related skincare creams.
Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis also contain carotenoids and triterpenoid saponins, which contribute to their ability to soothe and protect the skin during colds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in late morning when the dew has dried but the flowers are still moist.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer on paper bags or a drying rack for 7 to 10 days.
Infuse 2 cups of dried calendula officinalis flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2 to 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids then add beeswax.
Heat the infused oil mixture with 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler until the wax has melted and the mixture has cooled.

remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Elder cream contains antiviral compounds like sambunigrin, which helps to combat viral infections such as the common cold by inhibiting viral replication and reducing its spread throughout the body.

Anti-inflammatory agents: Elder cream's anti-inflammatory properties, particularly its flavonoids and anthocyanins, help to reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation associated with colds, making it a natural remedy for relieving congestion and sinus pressure.

Mucolytic properties: Elder cream contains mucolytic compounds that help break down mucus and reduce its thickness, making it easier to expel and relieving coughs, runny nose, and other respiratory symptoms characteristic of colds.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, creams helps with colds because of its high concentration of bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and anthocyanins. These natural extracts have potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe a sore throat and alleviate congestion. The cream's active ingredients work to reduce the severity and duration of cold symptoms, such as coughing, sneezing, and runny nose. By promoting a healthy immune response and reducing inflammation, Sambucus nigra creams provide fast and effective relief from cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with colds by acting as powerful antioxidants, reducing inflammation, and modulating the immune response to alleviate symptoms.
Vitexin: This flavonoid glycoside has antiviral properties, which can help inhibit the replication of viruses responsible for the common cold, thus reducing its severity and duration.

Sambunigrin: This saponin glycoside possesses immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help boost the body's immune response and reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines associated with colds.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in antiviral and antimicrobial compounds that help soothe and calm cold and flu symptoms.
Leaves: They contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and relieve cold symptoms.
Fruits: They are high in vitamin C and other antioxidants that help boost the immune system and fight off infections associated with colds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 stems of sambucus nigra flowers at full bloom and gently clean them with distilled water.
Infuse 2 cups of the cleaned sambucus nigra flowers in 4 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil.
Steep the infused oil mixture in a double boiler for 2-3 hours or overnight to allow for efficient extraction.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.
Whip the extracted oil mixture with shea butter and beeswax at a 1:1 ratio to create a smooth cream texture.

remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lemon verbena contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain viruses, including those responsible for the common cold, helping to reduce the severity and duration of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The creams may contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce congestion, coughing, and sore throats associated with colds by reducing swelling and inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Expectorant properties: Lemon verbena's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the risk of secondary infections.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Verbena officinalis, also known as lemon verbena, creams helps with colds because it contains antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that combat the underlying causes of respiratory issues. The cream's active compounds, such as linalool and limonene, work to soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and congestion. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cold. This natural remedy provides relief from cold symptoms, promoting a faster and more comfortable recovery.
remedy_constituents: Lignans: Lignans in Verbena officinalis creams have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate cold symptoms by reducing the severity and duration of viral infections.

Iridoids: Iridoids, specifically verbascoside, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can aid in fighting off infections that cause colds and reduce associated symptoms like congestion and coughing.

Rosmarinic acid (a phenolic compound): Rosmarinic acid, a polyphenol present in Verbena officinalis, possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat oxidative stress and inflammation associated with colds, promoting faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Verbena officinalis are used to make creams for colds because of their antiviral and antibacterial properties, which help alleviate symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Verbena officinalis are used for their expectorant properties, making them suitable for creams that help relieve congestion associated with colds.
Flowers: Flowers of Verbena officinalis are used in creams for colds due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help soothe respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning when the plant is at its peak fragrance and potency.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried verbena officinalis in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container to remove the plant material.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused liquid with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency then let it cool before transferring to a storage container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with colds because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe a sore throat and reduce congestion. The cream's antibacterial properties also help combat the underlying infection, while its expectorant qualities loosen phlegm and make it easier to cough up. Additionally, dandelion's anti-oxidant properties may help boost the body's immune system, allowing it to fight off the cold more effectively. By targeting multiple symptoms at once, dandelion creams can provide fast and effective relief from the discomfort of a cold.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion cream's antioxidant properties help combat free radicals caused by a cold virus, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress on the body.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion cream soothe sore throats, reduce swelling, and ease congestion associated with colds.
Expectorant properties: Dandelion's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to soothe sore throats and reduce congestion associated with colds.
Buds: The buds are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to boost the immune system and fight off infections caused by colds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers weighing approximately 50 grams.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder and filter through a fine-mesh sieve.
Mix 10 milliliters of coconut oil with 5 milliliters of beeswax and 20 grams of the powdered taraxacum officinale mixture.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 75 degrees celsius for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with colds because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as beta-carotene and lycopene, work to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses. Additionally, the cream's antioxidant properties help to combat the underlying viral or bacterial infections that cause the common cold. By soothing and calming the affected areas, damask rose creams provide relief from symptoms like coughing, sneezing, and stuffiness, allowing for a faster recovery from the cold.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Damask rose creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, sinuses, and throat, providing relief from cold symptoms such as stuffiness and sore throats.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of damask rose creams help protect the body against oxidative stress caused by a cold, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms like coughing and fatigue.

Mucolytic: Damask rose creams contain mucolytics that break down and thin out mucus in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and helping to alleviate congestion, coughing, and difficulty breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Damask rose roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing sore throats and relieving congestion.
Seeds: The seeds of the damask rose contain fatty acids, which help to moisturize and calm irritated skin, making them ideal for creams aimed at reducing nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa centifolia petals and dry them in a warm place for 3-4 days.
Combine 20g of dried rosa centifolia petals with 100ml of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to allow for infusion and extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container and discard solids after 1 hour.
Mix the strained oil with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil, heating until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with colds because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has potent antiviral and antibacterial properties. When applied topically, berberine can help soothe sore throats, reduce congestion, and combat infection-causing pathogens. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory agents may help alleviate sinus pressure and discomfort, making it easier to breathe and recover from a cold. By targeting both the physical and environmental factors associated with colds, goldenseal creams offer a natural and effective way to ease symptoms and support overall health.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Helps to combat viral infections, including the common cold, by inhibiting the replication of viruses and boosting the immune system's natural defenses.
Isoquinoline Alkaloids: Have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce congestion, sinus pressure, and other symptoms associated with a cold.

Berberastin: Has been shown to exhibit antiviral properties against the rhinovirus, which is responsible for many cases of the common cold, and may help to reduce the severity and duration of cold symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant are rich in berberine, a natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate congestion and soothe respiratory issues associated with colds.

Barks: The barks of goldenseal contain alkaloids like berberine and hydrastine, which have antiviral properties, making them effective against the common cold virus and helping to reduce its severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh golden seal root from your garden or local supplier ensuring proper identification and cleanliness.
Dry the harvested golden seal root in a warm place for several days or use a dehydrator on low setting for 24 hours.
Grind dried golden seal root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or electric coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered golden seal with distilled water and glycerin in a glass jar at a ratio of 1:2:0.5 grams per 100ml.
Infuse the mixture for 2-3 weeks by shaking the jar daily and then strain through cheesecloth or a coffee filter before filling sterilized jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with colds because they contain eucalyptus oil, a natural decongestant that helps to relieve congestion and sinus pressure. The cream's soothing properties also help to calm a sore throat and reduce coughing, making it easier to breathe and rest when you're feeling under the weather. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, further alleviating cold symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Tasmanian Blue Gum creams can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion.
Anti-inflammatory property: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and throat, alleviating discomfort and congestion associated with colds.

Antimicrobial property: Tasmanian Blue Gum has antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections often secondary to cold viruses, reducing the risk of complications and speeding up recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated skin, making them suitable for use in medicinal creams for cold-related skin issues.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and combat infection in the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from local sources or online suppliers.
Combine the harvested eucalyptus leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Infuse the mixture for at least two weeks by placing it in a warm dark place or using a low-heat stir plate for gentle agitation.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids after 24 hours of settling time.
Mix the strained oil with 200 grams of beeswax and heat it to 180°f while stirring until the mixture reaches a smooth consistent texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with colds because they contain compounds that have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, these compounds help to soothe and calm irritated sinuses and nasal passages, reducing congestion and sinus pressure associated with the common cold. Additionally, fennel's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe and promoting a faster recovery from illness. As a result, fennel creams provide relief from cold symptoms, helping to alleviate discomfort and promote overall wellness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Fennel's antimicrobial compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause cold symptoms, reducing the severity and duration of a cold.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory agents, like anethole and fenchone, reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, sinuses, and throat, alleviating common cold discomforts such as sinus pressure and coughing.
Expectorant properties: Fennel's expectorant compounds, including volatile oils, help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used to make medicinal creams because they contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated skin.
Barks: Fennel barks are used in medicinal creams for their antibacterial and antiviral properties, which help to combat cold-causing viruses and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare leaves in quantities of one ounce per batch.
Combine the harvested leaves with 16 ounces of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Infuse the mixture for 14 days by storing it in a cool dark place and shaking daily.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Mix one tablespoon of the infused oil with two tablespoons of beeswax and melt at 100 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Japanese honeysuckle creams helps with colds because of its unique combination of natural ingredients. The cream contains a blend of herbal extracts, including Japanese honeysuckle, which has been traditionally used in Eastern medicine to soothe and calm the respiratory system. This natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce congestion and ease coughs, while also providing a soothing and moisturizing effect on the skin. Additionally, the cream's antibacterial properties help to fight off infection-causing bacteria, promoting faster recovery from colds.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Japanese Honeysuckle contains antiviral compounds like ajugapinin, which helps to inhibit the replication of viral particles and reduce the severity of cold symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory agents: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and alleviate sinus pressure.
Mucolytic agents: Japanese Honeysuckle's mucolytic properties, like glycosides and saponins, break down excess mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Japanese honeysuckle roots are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help relieve congestion and coughing.

Barks: The barks of Japanese honeysuckle contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, making them effective in reducing inflammation and fighting off bacterial and viral infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry sufficient quantities of lonicera japonica flowers to yield desired amount of extract.
Combine 50 grams of dried flowers with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep mixture for 30 minutes, then strain liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 100 milliliters of the strained liquid and heat in a double boiler until melted and combined.
Pour warm mixture into sterile containers and allow to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with colds because of its potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The citral oil present in lemongrass has been shown to have a direct impact on the respiratory system, helping to reduce congestion and relieve coughing. Additionally, lemongrass's natural antibacterial qualities help to combat the underlying infection, allowing the body to recover faster from the cold. As a result, using herbal lemongrass creams can provide fast and effective relief for cold symptoms, making it a popular remedy in many traditional healing practices.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses that cause colds, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as limonene and beta-pinene, help reduce congestion and swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and alleviating sinus pressure.
Decongestant properties: The aroma of lemongrass has been shown to help dilate airways, reducing mucus production and relieving congestion, making it an effective natural remedy for colds and coughs.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing sore throats and reducing congestion.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of lemongrass have natural decongestant properties, helping to relieve nasal pressure and sinusitis symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of lemongrass are rich in vitamin C, which boosts the immune system and helps fight off cold viruses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh leaves of cymbopogon citratus and wash them thoroughly with distilled water to remove impurities.
Dry the washed leaves in shade for 24 hours or using a food dehydrator at low temperature to reduce moisture content.
Grind 10 grams of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 50 grams of beeswax, 20 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of shea butter in a heat-resistant glass bowl to create a smooth blend.
Heat the mixture over low temperature for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the ingredients melt together and form a creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with colds because of its natural decongestant properties, which can ease sinus pressure and relieve congestion. The menthol in peppermint also helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, while the calming effects of peppermint can soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing quick relief from cold symptoms.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: This compound has natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve congestion and open up airways, making it easier to breathe when you have a cold.
Menthol: Menthol's cooling and warming properties help to soothe a sore throat, ease coughs, and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing fast relief from cold symptoms.

Camphor: Camphor has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms, such as fever, chills, and body aches, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol, which helps to ease congestion and relieve coughs.
Leaves: Abundant in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties to combat cold symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves by cutting them from mature plants in the morning dew.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator set at 95°f for 6 hours or longer.
Combine 20 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar daily to release essential oils and flavors.
Strain the cream through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar discarding solids and storing the liquid in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with colds because of its natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, the cream's active ingredients, such as salve and thymol, work to soothe a sore throat, reduce congestion, and ease coughing fits. Additionally, sage's antimicrobial properties help combat viral infections that cause colds, promoting faster recovery and relief from symptoms like runny nose and sinus pressure. By targeting both the physical and emotional aspects of a cold, herbal sage creams provide a natural and effective solution for alleviating discomfort and speeding healing.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Sage contains antiviral compounds such as rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce the severity and duration of cold and flu symptoms by inhibiting the growth of viruses.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce congestion and swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from cold-related sinus pressure and discomfort.

Expectorant compounds: Sage contains expectorant compounds such as camphor and thujone, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and cough up phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have been traditionally used to soothe respiratory issues and calm coughs, making them a popular choice for medicinal creams.
Buds: Sage buds contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce congestion and ease sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and dry them according to your preferred method for two weeks.
Grind one ounce of dried salvia officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt four ounces of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat and stir in six ounces of coconut oil until fully incorporated.
Add the powdered salvia officinalis to the melted wax and oil mixture, stirring for five minutes to ensure even distribution.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool and set at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein creams helps with colds because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat and chest. The cream's expectorant qualities help loosen and clear mucus congestion, making it easier to expel and relieve respiratory symptoms such as coughing and wheezing. Additionally, mullein's antimicrobial properties combat viral and bacterial infections, reducing the duration and severity of colds.
remedy_properties: Mucus-thinning and expectorant properties: Mullein's saponins help to break down mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.
Anti-inflammatory and soothing properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in mullein reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, sinuses, and airways, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: Mullein's bioactive compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain viruses and bacteria that cause colds, helping to combat infection and promote recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and soothe respiratory issues associated with colds.
Flowers: Known for their mucilaginous properties, making them effective in creating a soothing, protective barrier on the skin to relieve congestion and cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbascum thapsus flowers at dawn, taking care not to bruise the delicate petals during collection.
Dry the harvested verbascum thapsus flowers in a warm, dark place for seven to ten days, or until they reach a crumbly texture.
Grind 20 grams of dried verbascum thapsus flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered verbascum thapsus with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture for 30 minutes to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for colds are topical remedies infused with medicinal herbs that help alleviate cold symptoms by reducing congestion, soothing sore throats, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal creams for colds include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Melaleuca alternifolia, Echinacea angustifolia, Sambucus nigra, Rosmarinus officinalis, and Salvia officinalis. If left untreated, colds can significantly impact daily life, causing fatigue, decreased productivity, and strained relationships due to prolonged coughing, sneezing, and congestion.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to colds that are treated with herbal creams are minor skin irritations, rashes, and itchiness caused by allergic reactions or environmental factors. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and inflammation. They also provide antiseptic and antibacterial properties to promote faster healing and prevent infection.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with colds is a blend of Echinacea and Peppermint. Echinacea is known for its immune-boosting properties, which can help to fight off the underlying infection. Meanwhile, Peppermint's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties can provide relief from congestion and soothe a sore throat. Adding a touch of Ginger and Thyme to the mix can further enhance its effects, as Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties and Thyme is known for its antimicrobial properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with colds because they contain the essential oils of Eucalyptus globulus, a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory. These oils, particularly eucalyptol, help to relieve congestion by thinning mucus and promoting drainage, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the cooling sensation of the cream can help to soothe sore throats and reduce coughing. As the eucalyptus oil is absorbed into the skin, it can also help to ease sinus pressure and congestion, providing temporary relief from cold symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This compound helps relieve cold symptoms by acting as a bronchodilator, allowing air to pass more easily through constricted airways.
α-Pinene: α-Pinene helps to open up airways and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and relieve congestion associated with colds.
Borned: Borned helps by acting as an expectorant, making it easier to cough up mucus and other debris that can clog airways during a cold.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are commonly used in creams for colds due to their high eucalyptol content, which provides decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are used in creams for colds due to their rich content of tannins and phenolic compounds, which possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are also used due to their ability to help break down mucus and provide temporary relief from congestion.
remedy_recipe: Infuse eucalyptus globulus leaves in a carrier oil by mixing 250 grams of leaves with 500 milliliters of oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infusion by decanting the liquid into a clean container through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Combine the infused oil with beeswax and shea butter to create a mixture with 30 percent oil content.
Heat the mixture by placing it in a double boiler and maintaining a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Pour the hot mixture into a mold and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with colds because they contain compounds that stimulate the immune system and increase the production of white blood cells, which fight off viral infections. The anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea help to reduce congestion and ease symptoms such as sore throats and coughs. By applying the cream topically, the active ingredients are absorbed into the skin, where they can quickly reach the affected areas, providing relief and promoting faster recovery from colds and flu.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating cold symptoms.
Caffeic Acid Derivatives: These phenolic compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, which help combat the viral and bacterial causes of colds.
Isoquinoline Alkaloids: These alkaloids have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can reduce the severity and duration of cold symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain bioactive compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation.
Roots: They are rich in glycosides and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, making them effective in treating colds and flu.
Leaves: They contain compounds like rosmarinic acid and chicoric acid, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate cold symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom and the air is warm.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a single layer on paper towels at room temperature for 7 days.
Combine 50g of dried echinacea purpurea with 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams helps with colds because they contain thymol, a natural antimicrobial agent that inhibits the growth of bacteria and viruses. The thymol in these creams has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing congestion and relieving respiratory issues associated with colds. Additionally, thymol's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. As a result, thymus vulgaris creams provide natural relief from the symptoms of colds, promoting faster recovery and a return to normal health.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as a natural antimicrobial agent, helping to combat bacterial infections that can cause cold symptoms.
Caryophyllene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and congestion associated with colds.
Rosmarinic acid: Demonstrates antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate cold symptoms and support the body's natural recovery process.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are used due to their high concentration of thymol, a compound known for its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems are used as they contain oils rich in thymol, which helps to soothe and calm the respiratory system during colds and coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from the garden by pinching off the top sets of leaves with scissors.
Steep 1 cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in 2 cups of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes.
Strain the thymus vulgaris infusion through a cheesecloth into a heatproof container to collect the liquid.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler to melt the mixture.
Combine the cooled thymus vulgaris infusion with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture to create the final cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with colds because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing a sore throat and reducing congestion. The cream's active ingredient, ginger, has natural decongestant and expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, ginger's antimicrobial properties help combat the underlying infection, while its warming sensation can help ease sinus pressure, promoting a more comfortable recovery from colds and flu. This natural remedy can provide relief and support during cold and flu season.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpenes have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and cough associated with colds.
Shogaols: These sesquiterpenes exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which help combat cold-causing pathogens and reduce inflammation in the body.
Phenolic acids: These compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate cold symptoms such as fever, sore throat, and body aches.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part of ginger for cold creams due to its high concentration of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Roots: Used in cold creams for their warming and soothing effects, which help to relieve congestion and coughs.
Buds: Employed in cold creams for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried zingiber officinale root and 100g of base cream containing 30% water and 60% oil.
Infuse the zingiber officinale root in 200ml of hot water for 20 minutes at 95°c to 100°c.
Steep the infused mixture for 30 minutes and then strain it to get 150ml of liquid extract.
Mix the liquid extract with 50g of glycerin and 10g of vitamin e oil to enhance stability and moisturizing properties.
Blend the final mixture and pour it into containers for storage and ready use within 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with colds because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The creams' natural moisturizing properties also help to reduce congestion and promote easier breathing. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the skin, preventing further irritation and infection. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from cold symptoms, helping to alleviate discomfort and promote overall well-being. Its natural properties make it a popular choice for cold relief.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the throat, alleviating cold symptoms such as sore throats and coughs.
Licoricidin: Exhibits antiviral properties, which can help combat viral infections that cause colds, such as the common cold and flu.
Licopyrones: Possesses anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce the severity of cold symptoms and support the body's immune response.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps to soothe and calm cold symptoms.

Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and other compounds that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful for skin creams to combat cold-related infections.

Flowers: The flowers are used for their sweet, licorice-like flavor and aroma, as well as their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in soothing cold symptoms and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Measure 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder into a clean glass container.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 5 grams of shea butter and 20 grams of distilled water to the double boiler mixture.
Stir in 10 grams of glycerin and 5 grams of vitamin e oil into the mixture.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before applying.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with colds because they contain tea tree oil, which has natural antiviral and antibacterial properties. This oil can help reduce the severity and duration of cold symptoms by targeting the underlying causes of the illness. By inhibiting the growth of viruses and bacteria, tea tree oil can help alleviate congestion, coughs, and sore throats associated with colds, promoting a faster recovery and relief from uncomfortable symptoms. Its soothing properties also help calm and comfort the skin.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These flavonoids help combat colds by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause infections.
Cineole: This terpene is an expectorant, which helps relieve congestion by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel, thus alleviating cold symptoms.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the severity of cold symptoms, such as sore throats and runny noses.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high amounts of eucalyptol, which is a key ingredient in many cold and flu remedies.
Barks: The barks are rich in eucalyptus oil, which has decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems also contain eucalyptol and other compounds that help to relieve respiratory issues associated with colds.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of distilled water and 250 grams of cold-pressed carrier oil in a double boiler.
Melt the carrier oil in the double boiler over low heat for approximately 30 minutes.
Combine 50 grams of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil with 250 grams of distilled water in a separate container.
Add the essential oil mixture to the melted carrier oil and mix well using a stainless steel spatula.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia creams helps with colds because they contain natural compounds that stimulate the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of cold symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea angustifolia also help to reduce congestion and alleviate sore throats. By applying the cream topically, the active ingredients can be absorbed directly into the skin, providing targeted relief and supporting the body's natural defenses against infection. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to a cold prevention and treatment regimen.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce congestion and alleviate cold symptoms.
Alkaloids (Cichoric and Isobutylamides): Alkaloids present in Echinacea angustifolia help to boost the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of colds.
Flavonoids and Phenolic Acids: These antioxidants have been found to possess antiviral properties, helping to inhibit the replication of viruses that cause colds, thus reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are used to make creams for colds because they contain a high concentration of echinacoside, a compound believed to have immune-boosting properties.
Roots: The roots are also used due to their content of alkylamides, which are known to have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Additionally, the roots contain isobutylamides, which have been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat cold-related infections.
remedy_recipe: Extract the roots of echinacea angustifolia using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio for 2 hours.
Filter the resulting liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to obtain a clear solution in 30 minutes.
Combine the filtered liquid with a base ingredient like beeswax and coconut oil in a 1:1 ratio by weight.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a water bath at 150-170°f for 30-60 minutes to achieve a smooth consistency.
Pour the cooled mixture into a clean container and store it in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams helps with colds because they contain a concentrated extract of elderberry, a natural antiviral agent. The elderberry in these creams works by inhibiting the replication of viruses that cause respiratory infections, such as the flu and common cold. By reducing the severity and duration of these infections, Sambucus nigra creams can help alleviate symptoms like congestion, coughing, and sore throats. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have antiviral properties that help to inhibit the spread of the virus responsible for colds and flu.
Phenolic acids (e.g., Salicylic acid): These acids exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help to alleviate symptoms associated with colds, such as congestion and fever.
Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants have been shown to have antiviral activity, reducing the severity and duration of cold symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are used to make creams for colds due to their high content of antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra are used to make creams for colds due to their ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues.
Buds: The buds of Sambucus nigra are used to make creams for colds due to their antiviral and antibacterial properties, which help to combat infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in a warm place for 2-3 weeks or until they reach 10% moisture.
Combine 250g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 20-30 minutes or until the beeswax and coconut oil have melted and combined with the sambucus nigra.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container and store it in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to allow it to mature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams helps with colds because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in rosemary, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, have decongestant and expectorant effects, which help relieve congestion and coughs. The herb's antioxidant properties also aid in reducing inflammation, making it easier to recover from colds. Additionally, rosemary's expectorant properties help bring up mucus, making it easier to expel and relieve sinus pressure and congestion. This makes rosemary creams a natural remedy for colds.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: It exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help in reducing the severity of cold symptoms and preventing infections.
Rosmarinic acid: This antioxidant compound has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the body's immune system and fight off the underlying causes of colds.
Caryophyllene: As a terpene with anti-inflammatory properties, it helps in reducing congestion, cough, and sore throat associated with colds.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain essential oils and antioxidants that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and relieving cold symptoms.
Flowers: They possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to combat infections and ease congestion associated with colds.
Stems: They are a rich source of rosmarinic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful in creams for cold relief.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g of dried rosmarinus officinalis flowers in 100ml of carrier oil at 40-60°c for 2-3 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Add 5g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter to the infused oil and melt at 60-70°c.
Stir in 5ml of distilled water and continue to heat until the mixture reaches 40-50°c.
Pour the mixture into a clean tin or jar and allow it to cool and solidify completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with colds because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, have been shown to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, Salvia officinalis' expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe. Its antiviral properties also help combat the underlying viral infection, supporting the body's natural recovery process and reducing the duration of cold symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory symptoms associated with colds.
Salvianolic acid B: This phenolic acid has potent antioxidant properties, which can help combat oxidative stress caused by the common cold virus.
Thujone: This terpene has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat bacterial and viral infections, as well as reduce congestion and cough associated with colds.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Salvia officinalis in creams for colds due to their high content of volatile oils, particularly thujone and camphor, which have decongestant and antimicrobial properties.
Buds: The buds are also used in creams for colds due to their high concentration of essential oils, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion.
Leaves: The leaves are again used in creams for colds due to their antiviral and antibacterial properties, which help to combat the underlying causes of colds and promote recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh salvia officinalis leaves from a clean environment at dawn when dew is still present.
Clean and dry the leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Infuse 20g of dried salvia officinalis in 100ml of cold-pressed vegetable oil for 2 to 3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter and 50ml of the infused oil in a double boiler at 160-180°f for 10-15 minutes.
